mission.title: cilium: Errors and warnings about identity release
mission.description: Errors and warnings about identity release. This issue affects 16+ users.

steps.title: Identify cilium troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cilium deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review cilium configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### What happened?

We are experiencing some identity release related errors and warnings ever since we've upgraded Cilium 1.12.2 to 1.12.4 (we are running 1.12.5

steps.title: Apply the fix for Errors and warnings about identity release
steps.description:
There have been multiple reports of excessive log pressure due to mistaken invocations of releaseCIDRIdentities with CIDRs that are mapped to a reserved identity. This is a first-pass fix for this overall issue by not logging in this case.

The logging issue, in one case, coincides with this issue
```yaml
The associated identity:
```

steps.title: Confirm Errors and warnings about identity release is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
There have been multiple reports of excessive log pressure due to mistaken invocations of releaseCIDRIdentities with CIDRs that are mapped to a reserved identity. This is a first-pass fix for this overall issue by not logging in this case.

The logging issue, in one case, coincides with this issue which has been fixed:  https://github.com/cilium/cilium/pull/23764

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
